description: SemVer 2.0.0 precedence + constraint selection modelled on the python-semanticversion library: compareV/versionLt/versionEq/satisfies/select over (major, minor, patch, pre-release identifier list, build metadata), with total-order axioms (swap/refl/transitivity, trichotomy, lt-irreflexive/transitive, eq an equivalence) plus the frozen SemVer field-precedence relation. The two load-bearing SemVer discriminators are pinned by spec: a pre-release version has strictly LOWER precedence than the same major.minor.patch without one (the empty pre-release list is GREATEST, the opposite of a naive list order), and build metadata is ENTIRELY ignored in precedence (full order-transparency against an arbitrary third version). The pre-release identifier rules are frozen-op-anchored through preCmp/identCmp (numeric below alphanumeric, numeric compared numerically, alphanumeric in ASCII order, longer set wins on equal prefix), and select is pinned by a witness clause (selected satisfies the constraint and is in the list) together with a MAXIMALITY clause (no satisfying member is strictly greater) and a UNIQUENESS-up-to-eq clause — jointly making select the unique greatest matching version. Scope: this models the already-PARSED SemVer precedence core (no version-string parser); constraints are desugared clause lists (Op ⋄ Version), the SimpleSpec surface syntax treated as grounded input. SemVer precedence is DISTINCT from PEP 440 (packaging_version): no epoch, no trailing-zero trimming, empty pre-release greatest, numeric identifiers below alphanumeric, build metadata dropped.

apis.name: select
apis.type: List Clause → List Version → Option Version
